title: Cordia loureiroi
intro: Cordia loureiroi, also known as Cordia or Cordia fruit, is a small deciduous tree or shrub native to China and Southeast Asia. The plant is valued for its numerous health benefits, including its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties, making it a popular natural remedy for various ailments. The bioactive constituents of Cordia loureiroi include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Cordia loureiroi include teas, capsules, and extracts, which are often used to support cardiovascular health and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and depression.
taxonomy: Cordia loureiroi is classified in the kingdom Plantae, and more specifically in the family Boraginaceae. This plant belongs to the order Lamiales and the genus Cordia. It is further divided into the species Cordia loureiroi. The classification is based on its morphological characteristics and genetic makeup. This classification helps to understand the evolutionary relationships between different species of the plant.
uses: Cordia loureiroi is known for its various medicinal uses, including its ability to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The plant is also used to alleviate symptoms of diarrhea and dysentery. In traditional medicine, Cordia loureiroi is used to treat various skin conditions, such as eczema and acne. Its bark and leaves are often used to make a decoction, which is consumed to treat different health problems. Additionally, the plant is also used to treat digestive issues, including indigestion and stomach pain.

Its leaves are believed to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help in reducing inflammation and improving overall health. The flavonoids present in Cordia loureiroi, such as kaempferol and quercetin, have been reported to exhibit hepatoprotective and cardio-protective effects.
